northStar: A visible, interruptible, personable Live2D desktop companion with recent context continuity.

frontend-full.ciTrigger: Runs on PRs that touch frontend-visible paths; non-frontend PRs keep the frontend-full check visible and pass it with an explicit skip message.
scope: transparent pet hit-test, drag, wheel, and pass-through
Settings editable fields, provider readiness, Test LLM, and active-chat rejection
Chat waiting/generating/stopped/failed/retry UI through provider failure and abort harnesses
Stop clearing speech playback, mouth-open state, and audio queue
Microphone voice input through ASR and the normal chat runtime
Decoded-audio waveform mouth movement during real TTS playback
Real OpenAI-compatible TTS bytes entering Electron renderer playback when credentials are supplied

features.title: Transparent pet window
acceptance: Electron starts a transparent always-on-top pet window.
The pet window has no OS title bar, browser chrome, visible page frame, or document scrollbars.
The renderer background is truly transparent; the user sees the desktop behind non-model pixels.
Transparent non-model pixels pass pointer events through to apps underneath unless an explicit visible control is present.
Visible model pixels remain interactive for touch, drag, and context commands when click-through is disabled.
The window can toggle pointer passthrough and can be dragged from the pet surface when unlocked.
Dragging moves the desktop pet/window rather than scrolling a page or only offsetting the Live2D model inside a stale invisible rectangle.
The renderer receives a typed preload bridge exposed as window.greyfield.

qa.script: pnpm test:frontend; pnpm harness:v1-visual; pnpm harness:electron

features.title: Live2D model import
acceptance: A .model3.json file or containing directory resolves to one model manifest.
The model3 manifest parser extracts moc, textures, expression names, and motion groups.
The desktop stage loads a real Live2D model through Pixi/Cubism.
The fallback preview is never counted as V1 Live2D acceptance.
The Live2D harness proves usedFallback=false with non-transparent WebGL pixels and frame changes.
Model scale and position settings affect the real stage and persist through settings IPC.

qa.script: vitest model-manifest model3-parser fallback-stage-frame live2d-deps; pnpm harness:live2d

features.title: Motion expression touch mapping
acceptance: Touch areas resolve deterministically with topmost-last ordering.
Expression and motion requests map through explicit fallback rules.
DigitalMate-style touch and emotion reactions are profile-driven, not hardcoded in the renderer.
Audio volume maps to bounded mouth-open values with a noise gate.

qa.script: vitest stage-config stage-driver interaction-profile audio-level-meter; pnpm harness:live2d

features.title: Model alpha hit test
acceptance: Visible model pixels are detected from the final rendered alpha channel.
Transparent Live2D canvas pixels are not treated as model hits.
The pet renderer emits model hit state changes for pointer movement.
Fallback stage exposes equivalent alpha hit semantics so dev mode does not lie.

features.title: Transparent-area pass-through
acceptance: Transparent area uses renderer alpha hit testing to request Electron mouse pass-through.
Visible model area requests passthrough=false when Model Pass Through is disabled.
Model Pass Through forces passthrough=true even on visible model pixels.
Electron main only applies setIgnoreMouseEvents/window movement and does not decide Live2D hit semantics.
Native BrowserWindow.setShape is not the default Windows V1 path because it visually clips animated Live2D and can drift native bounds.

qa.script: vitest pet-interaction desktop-runtime-bridge; pnpm harness:pet:quick; pnpm harness:v1-visual; pnpm harness:electron

features.title: Model drag window
acceptance: Left pointer down on visible model pixels starts a window drag.
Pointer move during drag updates only window x/y.
Native window width and height stay unchanged during drag.
Drag never changes live2d scale or model transform.
Pointer up/cancel releases drag and later pointer moves do not continue moving the window.

qa.script: vitest pet-interaction; pnpm harness:pet:quick; pnpm harness:electron

features.title: Bounded wheel scale
acceptance: Wheel scale only runs on visible model pixels.
Scale is clamped to the V1 range 0.4..2.0.
Wheel input is throttled.
Wheel scale is disabled during drag and when Model Pass Through is enabled.

qa.script: vitest pet-interaction; pnpm harness:pet:quick; pnpm harness:electron

features.title: Speech bubble placement
acceptance: Speech bubble is enabled by default and can be disabled from settings.
Bubble anchors to the model mask region, not the whole window rectangle.
Default placement uses a stable upper window slot instead of following model animation or transform movement.
Bubble flips to the left when the model is near the right edge and clamps horizontally and vertically inside the pet window and available screen viewport.
Bubble text normalizes whitespace and caps long replies so the pet bubble remains a short surface while full history stays in chat.
Bubble hit/shape rect stays stable while text is capped.
Electron long-reply harness proves first streaming token reaches the pet bubble, long text stays capped, the bubble remains in place, and Chat keeps the full assistant reply.

Electron edge/click-through harness proves a right-edge bubble remains inside the viewport/screen, transparent stage points stay outside native shape while the bubble is visible, disabling the bubble removes its native shape rect, and disabling the bubble during Model Pass Through keeps the stored native shape fresh before pass-through is turned off.

qa.script: vitest speech-bubble-placement speech-bubble-text pet-window-shape electron-check-helpers; pnpm harness:v1-visual; pnpm harness:electron; pnpm harness:electron:bubble-long-reply; pnpm harness:electron:bubble-edge-clickthrough

features.title: Streaming text runtime
acceptance: text.input streams assistant.text.delta before final output.
The turn is appended to session history.
Electron renderer can send a message and display the fake assistant reply.
Hosted Electron renderer never constructs real LLM providers; it sends runtime input to Electron main.
A newer text input interrupts the active main-process runtime before starting a new provider stream.
OpenAI-compatible provider timeouts and malformed stream data become readable runtime error state instead of crashing the renderer.
Runtime errors restore the failed user text into the chat draft so the user can retry without retyping.
Chat renders user-facing Waiting, Generating, Stopped, Failed, and Retry-ready states instead of raw runtime tokens.
Provider failures do not append half turns to the JSONL session.
Electron provider-failure harness covers missing-key, unauthorized, forbidden, not-found, timeout, and malformed-stream failures with visible chat errors and restored retry drafts.
Electron provider-abort harness proves Stop stays clickable during streaming, closes the active provider HTTP request, shows Stopped, and does not append the old partial assistant reply.
Settings can send a small LLM test request through Electron main and report first-token success or a readable failure.

qa.script: vitest runtime-loop desktop-runtime-bridge chat-status; pnpm test:backend; pnpm test:frontend; pnpm harness:acceptance; pnpm harness:v1-visual; pnpm harness:electron; pnpm harness:electron:provider-failure; pnpm harness:electron:provider-abort

features.title: Sentence-level TTS queue
acceptance: Complete sentences synthesize before the full assistant response is finished.
Partial sentence text remains buffered until punctuation or final flush.
Desktop voice output is disabled by default so the app does not suddenly speak.
Users can enable or disable assistant reply speech from settings.
When enabled with OpenAI-compatible TTS settings, sentence audio is synthesized through /audio/speech and played as real audio bytes.
Settings exposes Test Voice so users can validate TTS settings without sending a chat message.
Fake TTS bytes still fall back to local renderer speech output for offline harness coverage.
Chat exposes microphone voice input; browser recording bytes are sent through Electron main, ASR, transcript.final, and the same runtime text path as typed messages.
An independent draggable floating control window exposes text input, microphone input, voice output toggle, Settings, pass-through, hide, and Stop without interfering with pet-window transparency.
OpenAI-compatible ASR posts microphone audio to /audio/transcriptions while fake ASR keeps offline harness coverage deterministic.
Renderer mouth-open is driven during real audio playback from decoded PCM audio energy instead of a one-time compressed-byte estimate.
Finished renderer speech playback clears queued speech UI state across desktop windows.
TTS or speech playback failure reports a voice-only error and does not remove the assistant text reply or session turn.
Long replies are capped by a per-turn TTS character budget so playback cannot run unbounded.
Multiple assistant audio chunks are serialized so speech playback cannot overlap itself.
Stop cancels microphone listening, ASR/TTS runtime state, active renderer speech playback, queued speech UI state, and mouth-open state.
Audio runtime exposes pure RMS VAD, microphone recorder, and decoded PCM mouth-open timeline boundaries.

qa.script: vitest sentence-splitter vad tts-text-budget speech-synthesis-output microphone-recorder audio-level-meter openai-compatible-asr-provider openai-compatible-tts-provider runtime-loop runtime-service desktop-runtime-bridge config; pnpm harness:v1-visual; pnpm harness:electron:stop-audio; pnpm harness:electron:voice-input; optional provider proof: pnpm harness:real-tts; optional desktop provider proof: pnpm harness:electron:real-tts

features.title: Interrupt state machine
acceptance: runtime.interrupt stops later LLM chunks.
runtime.interrupt does not emit a final assistant message for the stopped partial reply.
Desktop bridge clears pending assistant draft, TTS queue, and mouth-open state.
Electron renderer Stop button drives the interrupt path.
Main-process runtime aborts the active OpenAI-compatible request signal.
Concurrent text inputs do not leave older provider streams alive.
Electron stop-audio harness proves Stop cancels active speech playback, clears the speech queue, and resets mouth-open state.

qa.script: vitest runtime-loop desktop-runtime-bridge runtime-service interrupt; pnpm harness:electron:provider-abort; pnpm harness:electron:stop-audio

features.title: Core persona and memory prompt assembly
acceptance: Core prompt assembly includes character persona, boundaries, memory, handoff, and recent turns.
Core recent context injection is capped to the configured turn budget.
This feature proves package-level prompt assembly only; desktop restart continuity is tracked separately.

features.title: Settings shell
acceptance: Settings state exposes provider model, voice, microphone, character file, and model path.
Settings edits do not mutate conversation history.
Settings persist to greyfield.config.json through Electron IPC.
Settings shows provider readiness as Preview, blocked, or ready-to-test before users run Test LLM.
Settings exposes a Test LLM action and renders first-token success or a readable failure without writing chat history.
Settings provider/Test LLM harness proves fake Preview, missing Base URL/API key/model reasons, blocked states send no provider requests, testing, success, and failure states.
Electron settings-active-chat harness proves Test LLM is rejected with visible Stop-or-wait guidance while chat is actively streaming and does not send an extra provider request.
V1 visual acceptance proves Settings Window scale/position controls remain readable and usable at narrow desktop widths.

qa.script: vitest desktop-runtime-bridge settings-provider-status settings-test-llm config settings-controller runtime-service; pnpm harness:v1-visual; pnpm harness:electron; pnpm harness:electron:settings-provider-test; pnpm harness:electron:settings-active-chat-test

features.title: Desktop persistent recent context
acceptance: Electron main runtime loads the configured character persona from characters/greyfield.yaml.
Electron main runtime loads local memory from data/memory.md.
Electron main runtime appends user and assistant turns to a JSONL SessionStore instead of an in-memory session only.
A restart harness proves recent conversation context is available after app restart.
Successful turns are persisted before the final assistant message is emitted to the renderer, so app shutdown after final UI does not drop the assistant turn.
Recent context remains capped and inspectable so prompt growth is bounded.

qa.script: vitest runtime-service character-persona jsonl-session-store prompt-assembler; pnpm harness:electron:restart-context

features.title: Fake provider acceptance chain
acceptance: Fake LLM, ASR, and TTS run without network credentials.
Acceptance verifies text -> LLM -> TTS runtime event flow.
Acceptance verifies fake audio input -> ASR -> transcript -> LLM -> TTS without microphone hardware.
